query:
You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository.
The codebase is at version 10.0 (commit: 4a30419e).

## Issue to Resolve

Always mark read when archiving email messages
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same feature request.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.
- [X] This issue only contains a request for one single feature, **not** multiple (related) features.

### App version

6.804

### Problem you are trying to solve

I'd love some way to mark things as read when archiving.

Generally, when archiving something unread, it's because I have sufficient information from the subject line/already roughly know the contents - or know I don't care too much about it, but still want it "just in case". I like being able to filter by unread though, especially if it's something I want to get back to later and refile - but don't want in my inbox because it's not really a priority. 

### Suggested solution

An option to mark things read when archiving, like what's done with spam, would be great. Alternatively I think it'd be similarly nice to have one of the possible swipe actions be "mark as read and archive" together.

### Screenshots / Drawings / Technical details

_No response_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Honestly I think this should be the default. I can't think of a use case to archive a message and not also mark it read.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `legacy/core/build.gradle.kts`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/ArchiveOperations.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/KoinModule.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/MessagingController.java`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
